seo.title: French Retro Promotional Video Maker | Vintage Brand Ad Style
seo.description: Generate elegant French retro promo videos with black-and-white tones, film grain texture, and cinematic pacing for brand storytelling and product campaigns.

hero.title: French Retro Style Promotional Video
hero.description: Transform one reference image into a vintage brand film with French editorial tone. This workflow adds black-and-white grading, film grain texture, and restrained cinematic movement for premium campaigns.
hero.inputPlaceholder: Upload your product or model image
hero.outputImageAlt: French retro-style black-and-white promotional video still

steps.title: Upload a reference image
steps.description: Start from a product shot or fashion reference. The workflow treats it as the visual anchor for styling.

steps.title: Apply retro styling
steps.description: Use AI image editing to push a French vintage look: monochrome contrast, film grain, and subtle imperfections.

steps.title: Generate multiple promo clips
steps.description: Create variations of slow, editorial movements such as walking shots, close-ups, and cinematic stillness—optimized for social ads.

steps.title: Export for campaigns
steps.description: Download your final clips and use them on TikTok/Reels, landing pages, or brand campaigns.

items.question: What is the French Retro Style Promotional Video workflow?
items.answer: It’s an AI workflow that turns a reference image into cinematic, vintage-style promotional videos with a French editorial vibe.

items.question: What inputs work best?
items.answer: Clean product shots or fashion references with clear subjects. Higher resolution inputs generally produce more stable details.

items.question: Can I control the mood and pacing?
items.answer: Yes. You can edit the prompts to make it more dramatic, softer, or more energetic, and control shot length via video node settings.

items.question: Is this suitable for ads?
items.answer: Yes. It’s optimized for short-form ad formats and premium brand storytelling.

items.question: What models are used?
items.answer: This workflow uses a combination of image editing and video generation models (including Veo 3.1) to produce cinematic results.

items.question: Can I use the output commercially?
items.answer: In general, yes—outputs can be used commercially, but you should follow model provider terms and ensure your input assets are licensed.
